explanation: The PDF rule set: do not modify time into the past, only modify into the future; back up CVM configuration; check for time-sensitive apps; turn off VM time sync and shut down the VMs; then change NTP/CVK time; finally start the VMs and restore service.

question: Among the following descriptions of CAS HA / cluster commands, which one is INCORRECT?
options: cha cluster-list obtains a list of clusters managed by the HA process.
cha cluster-status cluster-id queries status of a specified cluster.
cha node-list cluster-id queries a list of hosts in a cluster.
cha node-restart host-name restarts a single host in the cluster.
explanation: The PDF lists cha cluster-list, cha cluster-status, cha node-list, and cha node-status as the documented HA commands. There is no 'cha node-restart' command in the PDF.

question: Regarding the recommended way to modify the root password on a CAS host, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?
options: Log in to the CAS platform, select the host, right-click it, and select Edit Host to modify the root password.
Logging in to the CVK background through SSH to modify the root password is the recommended best practice.
Modifying root via SSH can destroy the confidentiality-free relationship between the CVM and the CVK.
Improper root password change can make the CVK display abnormally in the CVM foreground.
explanation: The PDF says it is NOT recommended to log in to the CVK background through SSH to modify the root password; doing so destroys the CVM/CVK confidentiality-free relationship and may cause abnormal CVK display in the CVM foreground.

question: Which of the following statements about CAS online upgrade prerequisites are INCORRECT? (Select all that apply.)
options: The cluster to which the host to be upgraded belongs must contain other hosts.
The cluster to which the CVM host belongs must contain hosts that are NOT running CVM.
Running VMs may use local non-shared storage during the online upgrade.
Online upgrade requires that all VMs in the cluster be powered off before the upgrade starts.
Offline upgrade allows the choice of manual shutdown or auto shutdown of VMs.
explanation: The PDF states online upgrade requires the running VMs to use shared storage (otherwise upgrade will fail) and never demands all VMs be powered off; that requirement belongs to offline upgrade. The other statements match the documented prerequisites.

question: When the system stops executing scheduled tasks after the management host's system time is edited, which of the following troubleshooting steps is WRONG?
options: Log in to the management host through SSH.
Execute service tomcat8 restart to restart the tomcat daemon.
Execute service casserver restart to restart the casserver daemon.
Continue performing other operations in the CVM system immediately after issuing the restart command, since CVM remains fully available.
explanation: The PDF explicitly warns that the CVM system becomes unavailable after the restart command is executed, all tasks must be stopped beforehand, and no operations should be performed during the procedure.

question: Regarding CAS log files inside the CVK var directory, which of the following descriptions are INCORRECT? (Select all that apply.)
options: Ocfs2_shell_XX.log is the script log of the shared file system.
HA log is self-developed and is included in the CVK var directory.
vSwitch log records commands manually executed in the background.
The CVK var directory contains no VM-related log files.
fsm storage manager log is excluded from the CVK var directory.
explanation: The PDF lists HA log (self-developed), Ocfs2_shell_XX.log, VM related log, vSwitch log, fsm storage manager log, and 'Log manually executed in the background' as items in the CVK var directory; statements 4 and 5 contradict this.

question: Regarding the performance optimization configuration ideas described for CAS, which of the following statements are INCORRECT? (Select all that apply.)
options: HugePages should be enabled at the host first, then enabled at the VM.
Disk provision should choose 'Lazy Zeroed' as the recommended performance optimization option.
Disk provision should choose 'Eager Zeroed' as the recommended performance optimization option.
HugePages must be enabled on the VM first; the host-side configuration is unnecessary.
Configure I/O Priority and Configure CPU Model are listed performance-optimization items.
explanation: The PDF specifies Disk Provision should choose 'Lazy Zeroed' (not Eager Zeroed) and that HugePages must be enabled on the host first and then on the VM (not the other way around). The other statements match documented optimization items.

question: Regarding the data center evolution trend from Bare Metal to Virtualization to Container as described in the course, which of the following statements is correct?
options: In the Bare Metal stage, every application uses its own Hypervisor running directly above the HostOS to achieve isolation.
Compared with Bare Metal, the Virtualization stage brings higher resource utilization, higher system availability, better compatibility, faster go business and simpler operation.
The Container stage relies on a Hypervisor to emulate a full GuestOS for each container in order to achieve isolation.
Bare Metal is described as having higher resource utilization than the Virtualization stage because it removes the GuestOS overhead.
explanation: The 'Evolution trend of Data Center' slide lists Bare Metal weaknesses (uneven resource utilization, upgrade difficulty, management complexity), and explicitly attributes higher resource utilization, higher system availability, better compatibility, faster go business and simpler operation to the Virtualization stage.

question: According to the 'What is virtualization' page, which of the following statements about virtualization are correct?
options: The objects of virtualization are various resources.
Virtualized logical resources hide unnecessary details from users.
Users can implement some or all functions of a real environment in a virtual environment.
Virtualization is restricted to consolidating x86 servers and cannot apply to networks or storage.
Virtualization requires every workload to expose all underlying hardware details directly to the end user.
explanation: The 'What is virtualization' page lists exactly three points: objects of virtualization are various resources; virtualized logical resources hide unnecessary details from users; users can implement some or all functions of a real environment in a virtual environment.

question: Regarding the cloud computing development timeline shown in the course, which of the following statements is correct?
options: VMware launched VMware Workstation in 2003, the same year the Xen project launched version 1.0.

In 2006, Google CEO Eric Schmidt first proposed the concept of 'cloud computing' at the Search Engine Strategies Conference & Expo, and Amazon provided EC2, S3, SimpleDB and other cloud services in the form of web services.
The OpenStack open source project was started in 2013 and the first version was named Docker.
Kubernetes 1.0 was released in 2010 and the CNCF was established the same year as the first project of K8s.
explanation: The cloud-computing timeline page places Eric Schmidt's first 'cloud computing' proposal at the Search Engine Strategies Conference & Expo and Amazon's EC2/S3/SimpleDB launch in 2006. VMware Workstation is 1999, Xen 1.0 is 2003, OpenStack Austin is 2010, and Kubernetes 1.0 / CNCF establishment is 2015.

question: Among the industry-cloud examples enumerated under the 'Four deployment models of cloud computing' slide, which of the following choices matches the slide exactly?
options: Industry cloud, Financial cloud, Medical cloud, Education cloud, Government cloud, Traffic cloud
Industry cloud, Financial cloud, Medical cloud, Manufacturing cloud, Government cloud, Traffic cloud
Industry cloud, Financial cloud, Telecom cloud, Education cloud, Government cloud, Logistics cloud
Industry cloud, Retail cloud, Medical cloud, Education cloud, Government cloud, Energy cloud
explanation: The 'Four deployment models' slide enumerates exactly Industry cloud, Financial cloud, Medical cloud, Education cloud, Government cloud and Traffic cloud. Manufacturing, Telecom, Logistics, Retail and Energy are not on the listed set.

question: Among the following descriptions of the 'Products and solutions of cloud computing' slide, which one is incorrect?
options: CRM is shown at the SaaS layer of the slide.
Azure, CloudSystem and Intercloud are shown in the cloud-service area of the slide.
H3Cloud, BlueCloud and FusionSphere are listed at the cloud infrastructure / IaaS layer.
OpenStack and Kubernetes are explicitly shown as the only two products on the slide.
explanation: The 'Products and solutions of cloud computing' slide enumerates CRM at SaaS, App Engine and EC2 at the PaaS/IaaS layer, Azure, CloudSystem and Intercloud in the Cloud service area, and H3Cloud, BlueCloud and FusionSphere at the cloud infrastructure / IaaS layer. OpenStack and Kubernetes do not appear on this slide.

question: Which of the following items are listed in the course's 'Value of virtualization technology' slide?
options: Efficient utilization through resource reuse and automatic scheduling
Agility with quick response, flexible elastic scheduling policies and self-adaptive service burst access traffic
Scalability that breaks the traditional vertical shaft architecture for scale-out and scale-up
Management cost saving through fewer maintenance devices and centralized maintenance platforms
Mandatory replacement of all Windows Server licenses with open-source GuestOS
explanation: The 'Value of virtualization technology' slide explicitly names Efficient utilization, Agility, Scalability and Management cost saving along with High-availability guarantee, Hardware cost saving, Software cost saving and Power cost saving. There is no claim of mandatory replacement of Windows Server licenses.

question: According to the 'Products compared with VMWare' slide, which CAS / H3C component maps to vCenter and ESXi respectively?
options: CVK maps to vCenter and CVM maps to ESXi.
CVM maps to vCenter and CVK maps to ESXi.
CloudOS maps to vCenter and ONEStor maps to ESXi.
UIS maps to vCenter and SDN/NFV maps to ESXi.
explanation: The 'Products compared with VMWare' slide pairs CAS CVM with vCenter (management) and CAS CVK with ESXi (hypervisor host), while ONEStor pairs with vSAN, SDN/NFV/UIS-Sec with NSX and CloudOS/UIS-Cloud with the vRealize Suite.

question: Which of the following capabilities are listed under '01|Resource manageability-overview' in the course?
options: Create VM in two steps
Dynamic resource scheduling
Dynamic power management
Affinity and non-affinity
Mandatory ESXi co-management
explanation: The Resource manageability overview slide lists Create VM in two steps, Dynamic resource scheduling, Dynamic power management, Affinity and non-affinity, Online/Offline migration, plus VM Group and Adjustment capability. ESXi co-management is not a listed item on this slide.

question: Among the following descriptions of the CAS GPU/vGPU feature, which is incorrect?
options: It supports GPU/vGPU resource pool management, including exclusive use and priority preemption for business.
Supported GPU models listed include K2000, M3000SE, M60, P40, P100 and RTX6000.
Supported vGPU models listed include M6, M60, P4, P100, V100, T4 and RTX6000.
GPU resources can only be statically bound at VM creation time and cannot be released automatically by any policy.
explanation: The GPU/vGPU slide states virtual machines can automatically allocate and release GPU/vGPU resources according to policies, contradicting option D. The listed GPU and vGPU models in B and C match the slide, as does the exclusive/priority-preemption capability in A.

question: Which of the following components or services are explicitly shown between the Active and Standby CVMs in the CAS high reliability architecture diagram?
options: Main communication
Hot standby service
Data exchange service that synchronizes configuration changes to the database in real time
Host heartbeat message channel
External S3 object replication service
explanation: The CAS high reliability architecture diagram pairs Active and Standby H3C CAS CVMs with Main communication, Hot standby service, Data exchange service (synchronize configuration changes to the database in real time) and a Host heartbeat message channel. There is no external S3 object replication service in the diagram.

question: Regarding the CAS '04|Strong security: full-system virtualized security architecture', which of the following statements are incorrect? (Select all that apply)
options: The four pillars of the architecture are Management standardization, Resource abstraction, Data centralization and Service diversification.
Two-factor authentication and management-platform access control are listed under the management-standardization pillar.
Distributed dual-stack virtual firewall is listed as an agent-based control under the service-diversification pillar.
VM disk encryption is shown under the data-centralization pillar against confidential data leakage.
Agentless antivirus and agentless intrusion detection / intrusion prevention are absent from the architecture.
explanation: The security architecture slide explicitly labels the distributed dual-stack virtual firewall, antivirus and intrusion detection/prevention as agentless under the service-diversification pillar, so option C (calling the firewall agent-based) and option E (denying the agentless antivirus/IDS are present) are both incorrect. Options A, B and D match the slide.

question: Regarding '05|The Best Alternative to VMware virtualization' as presented in the course, which of the following statements is correct?
options: The CVM virtualization management platform can uniformly manage both VMware and CAS virtual machines, with One-Click Migration of VMware VMs to CAS and Seamless DR backup/restore of VMware VMs to CAS.
VMware vCenter is required to remain the unified management plane and the CAS CVM is decommissioned during migration.
Seamless DR only protects CAS-native VMs and explicitly excludes VMware VMs.
One-Click Migration is unidirectional from CAS to VMware vSphere only.
explanation: The slide states the CAS CVM management system provides Unified Management of VMware and CAS VMs, One-Click Migration of VMware VMs to CAS, and Seamless DR by backing up and restoring VMware VMs to CAS for data protection and emergency takeover.

question: Among the following one-click capabilities, which is NOT listed under 'Scenario O&M' in the CAS operation and maintenance capability framework?
options: One-click health check
One-click resources analyzation
One-click storage space clear
One-click hypervisor kernel recompile
One-click zombie VMs removed
explanation: The 'Operation and maintenance capability framework' slide lists under Scenario O&M: One-click health check, One-click resources analyzation, One-click storage space clear, One-click resources statistics gathering, One-click VMs restore and One-click zombie VMs removed. There is no 'one-click hypervisor kernel recompile' item.

question: Regarding the calculation of the CPU main frequency, which of the following formulas is correct?
options: Main Frequency of CPU = External Frequency + Frequency Multiplication Factor.
Main Frequency of CPU = External Frequency x Frequency Multiplication Factor.
Main Frequency of CPU = Internal Frequency x Number of Cores.
Main Frequency of CPU = Bus Frequency / Frequency Multiplication Factor.
explanation: Per the Key parameters of CPU page, the main frequency (also called clock frequency, unit MHz) is computed as Main Frequency of CPU = External Frequency x Frequency Multiplication Factor.

question: Among the following descriptions of the L1 Cache of the CPU, which is correct?
options: The L1 Cache is composed of dynamic RAMs and is shared between cores.
The L1 Cache is composed of static RAMs and is divided into the L1d (data) cache and the L1i (instruction) cache, usually 32-256 K.
The L1 Cache can reach 10 M or more and is used to reduce memory latency.
The L1 Cache runs at half the speed of the main frequency for external chip implementations.
explanation: The Key parameters of CPU page states the L1 Cache (first-level cache) is composed of static RAMs and is divided into the data cache (L1d Cache) and the instruction cache (L1i Cache), with the highest speed and smaller capacity, usually 32-256 K. The 10 M or more capacity describes L3 Cache; the half-main-frequency speed describes the external-chip L2 Cache.

question: On the Xeon E5-2600v3 two-path platform based on Haswell-EP, which of the following statements about the QPI rates supported between two CPUs is correct?
options: Two CPUs are interconnected through QPI, supporting different rates of 9.6 Gbps and 8 Gbps.
Two CPUs are interconnected through QPI, supporting a fixed rate of 6.4 Gbps.
Two CPUs are interconnected through PCIe 3.0 lanes at 16 Gbps each.
Two CPUs are interconnected through DDR4 channels, with three DIMMs per channel.
explanation: Per the CPU architecture page (Xeon E5-2600v3, two-path, based on the Haswell-EP architecture), two CPUs are interconnected through QPI, supporting different rates: 9.6 Gbps and 8 Gbps.

question: Regarding the one-way bandwidth of a PCI-E 3.0 x8 card, which of the following values is correct?
options: 4 GB/s
8 GB/s
16 GB/s
2 GB/s
explanation: The PCI-E card rate comparison table lists PCI-E 3.0 x8 one-way bandwidth as 8 GB/s. (PCI-E 1.X x8 is 2 GB/s, PCI-E 2.X x8 is 4 GB/s, PCI-E 3.0 x16 is 16 GB/s.)

question: Among the following descriptions of SATA3 and SAS 2.0 high-speed transmission rates, which is correct?
options: SATA3 supports 6 Gbit/s and SAS 2.0 supports 12 Gbit/s.
SATA3 supports 3 Gbit/s and SAS 2.0 supports 6 Gbit/s.
Both SATA3 and SAS 2.0 currently support the high-speed transmission rate of 6 Gbit/s.
SATA3 supports 6 Gbit/s and SAS 2.0 supports 3 Gbit/s.
explanation: Per the SATA hard disk page, SATA3 currently supports the high-speed transmission rate of 6 Gbit/s; per the SAS hard disk page, SAS 2.0 currently supports the high-speed transmission rate of 6 Gbit/s.

question: Regarding the UEFI vs Legacy BIOS boot partition limits, which of the following statements is correct?
options: UEFI GPT is limited to 2.2 TB and Legacy BIOS MBR is limited to 9.4 ZB.
UEFI GPT is limited to 9.4 ZB and Legacy BIOS MBR is limited to 2.2 TB.
Both UEFI GPT and Legacy BIOS MBR are limited to 2.2 TB.
UEFI GPT has no limit and Legacy BIOS MBR is limited to 4 TB.
explanation: The Comparison of UEFI and Legacy BIOS page states UEFI GPT is limited to 9.4 ZB and BIOS MBR is limited to 2.2 TB.

question: Among the following descriptions of the DHCP lease renewal mechanism, which is correct?
options: The DHCP client first attempts to renew when the use time reaches 50% of the lease term, and again at 87.5% of the lease term if needed.
The DHCP client first attempts to renew when the use time reaches 25% of the lease term, and again at 75% of the lease term.
The DHCP client attempts to renew only once, at 100% of the lease term.
The DHCP client renews automatically every 60 seconds during the lease term.
explanation: The DHCP lease renewal page shows the first renewal request is sent when use time reaches 50% of the lease term, with another at 87.5% of the lease term if the first is not acknowledged.

question: Regarding the FC-1 layer of the FC protocol, which of the following statements is correct?
options: FC-1 specifies transmission mechanisms including frame format and information exchange between nodes.
FC-1 defines the transmission protocol including serial decoding, encoding, and error control, and has a specific link control function.
FC-1 defines the protocol mapping relationship between the bottom-level FC and high-level protocols such as IP or SCSI.
FC-1 provides public services with advanced features, namely the structure protocol and flow control between ports.
explanation: The FC protocol layer description lists FC-1 (Byte Coding Layer) as defining serial decoding, encoding, and error control, with a specific link control function. Frame format/information exchange is FC-2, high-level mapping is FC-4, and public services is FC-3.

question: Among the following descriptions of the FC address space, which is correct?
options: The fixed WWN is an 8-bit address space and the dynamic FC address is 64-bit.
The fixed WWN is a 64-bit address defined by the vendor in IEEE format; the dynamic FC address is 8/24-bit, locally generated.
Both the fixed WWN and the dynamic FC address are 24-bit.
The fixed WWN is 32-bit and the dynamic FC address is 16-bit, exchanged via LACP.
explanation: The FC address page states the fixed address (WWN) is 64-bit, defined by the vendor in IEEE format, while the dynamic address is 8/24-bit, locally generated, dynamically obtained when registering to the fiber channel network.

question: Regarding the NAS storage architecture, which of the following statements is correct?
options: NAS connects the storage device to the server through a direct SCSI cable in block mode.
NAS connects the storage device to the existing network and supports network file sharing protocols CIFS and NFS.
NAS uses the FCP protocol over a Fibre Channel network to access data in block mode.
NAS uses the iSCSI protocol to access data in block mode over Ethernet.
explanation: The Storage architecture--NAS page defines NAS as connecting the storage device to the existing network to provide data and file services, and supporting network file sharing protocols CIFS and NFS. Block-mode access is SAN (FC SAN or IP SAN), not NAS.

question: Which of the following statements about ACL identification serial number ranges are correct?
options: The basic ACL serial number range is 2000-2999.
The advanced (extended) ACL serial number range is 3000-3999.
The Layer-2 ACL serial number range is 4000-4999.
The user-defined ACL serial number range is 5000-5999.
The basic ACL serial number range is 1000-1999.
explanation: The ACL identification table lists basic ACL 2000-2999, extended ACL 3000-3999, Layer-2 ACL 4000-4999, and user-defined ACL 5000-5999. Option E contradicts the basic ACL range.

question: Which of the following are listed advantages of the SSD hard disk?
options: High data access speed.
Shock-proof, drop-proof.
No noise, low heat.
Wide operating temperature range and low weight.
Unlimited write life.
explanation: The SSD hard disk page lists Advantages as: high data access speed, shock-proof/drop-proof, no noise/low heat, wide operating temperature range, low weight. 'Limited write life' is explicitly listed as a Disadvantage, not unlimited.

question: The following are the three DHCP address assignment modes specified in the DHCP introduction:
options: Manual assignment, where the administrator binds fixed IP addresses to specific hosts.
Automatic assignment, where the host uses the address for a long time after assignment.
Dynamic assignment, where the address is leased and must be reapplied after the lease expires.
Stateful relay assignment, in which a DHCP relay leases the IP on behalf of the client.
Reservation assignment, an IPv6-only mode for stateful clients.
explanation: The DHCP address assignment mode page lists exactly three: Manual assignment, Automatic assignment, and Dynamic assignment. 'Stateful relay' and 'Reservation' are not modes defined in the PDF.

question: Which of the following statements about the QoS functions are correct?
options: Try to avoid network congestion and reduce the packet loss rate.
Provide dedicated bandwidth for specific users or specific services and support real-time services on the network.
Effectively manage bandwidth when congestion cannot be avoided and regulate IP network traffic.
QoS can create extra bandwidth on demand when the link is congested.
QoS can only be applied to the incoming direction of the interface and only takes effect for IP packets.
explanation: The QoS functions slide lists congestion avoidance, packet-loss reduction, bandwidth management when congested, traffic regulation, dedicated bandwidth, and real-time support. It explicitly states QoS cannot create bandwidth, only allocate it more reasonably. The QoS LR slide places LR at the link layer (not network layer) and applies it only to the outgoing direction (not incoming).

question: Which of the following statements about RAID effective-capacity formulas are correct? (n = number of disks, c = capacity per disk)
options: RAID 0 effective capacity = n x c.
RAID 1 effective capacity = c.
RAID 5 effective capacity = (n-1) x c.
RAID 6 effective capacity = (n-2) x c.
RAID 10 effective capacity = n x c (no mirroring loss).
explanation: The RAID advantage--Increase capacity page lists: RAID 0 capacity n x c, RAID 1 capacity c, RAID 5 capacity (n-1) x c, RAID 6 capacity (n-2) x c, RAID 10 capacity (n/2) x c. Option E omits the mirroring loss.

question: Which of the following statements about the ROW (Redirect On Write) snapshot are correct?
options: The source volume is frozen during the snapshot.
A new write to the source disk is stored to the log (and indexed), and the source is referenced when reading the snapshot.
The write operation of the source volume is basically not affected.
When the snapshot is canceled, all write logs are executed so the data status is updated synchronously with the source volume.
ROW reads the current data of the target address first and stores it before the new write, identical to COW.
explanation: The Comparison of COW and ROW table lists ROW: source frozen, new writes stored to a log (with index), snapshot reads refer to source volume, write path basically unaffected, and on cancel all write logs are executed. Option E describes COW behavior, not ROW.

question: Which of the following are listed as values or typical applications of NTP and snapshot in the PDF?
options: NTP is used to perform clock synchronization on devices on the network.
Typical NTP applications include log information records, debugging records, accounting systems, and cooperative processing of multiple systems.
Snapshot values include quick backup/restore, storing multiple recovery point objectives, and redefining data usage.
NTP supports password verification only in broadcast mode.
Snapshot is defined by SNIA as a partially available copy that excludes data at the start time.
explanation: The Introduction to NTP page lists clock-sync purpose plus the typical applications listed in option B. The Definition/values of snapshot page lists option C verbatim. The NTP verification page does not restrict password verification to broadcast mode, and SNIA defines snapshot as a fully available copy at a specific time point (not partial).

question: Which of the following are listed minimal-configuration disk counts in the RAID comparison table?
options: RAID 0: minimum 1 disk.
RAID 1: minimum 2 disks.
RAID 5: minimum 3 disks.
RAID 6: minimum 4 disks.
RAID 10: minimum 4 disks.
explanation: The Comparison of various RAID levels table lists minimal configurations of 1, 2, 4, 3, 4 for RAID 0, 1, 10, 5, 6 respectively. All five options match the table values exactly.

question: Among the following statements about the FC FLOGI and PLOGI communication process, which is incorrect?
options: The server and the storage are registered to the network through FLOGI (Fabric Login).
The server and the storage are registered to the name server through PLOGI (Port Login).
Session permission is obtained through PLOGI.
PLOGI uses the well-known fabric service address x'FF FFFE' and FLOGI uses x'FF FFFC'.
explanation: The FC protocol communication process page assigns the well-known address x'FF FFFE' to the Register (fabric) server (used by FLOGI) and x'FF FFFC' to the name server (used by PLOGI). Option D inverts the addresses.

question: Regarding the storage performance index IOPS and bandwidth, which of the following statements is wrong?
options: IOPS refers to the maximum I/O frequency that the system can process per unit of time.
Bandwidth refers to the flow rate per unit of time, expressed in MB/s.
For a storage with bandwidth 10 MB/s and IOPS 1000, writing 1000 1 KB files takes 1 second and IOPS has a high possibility of becoming the bottleneck.
For the same storage, writing one 1 MB file takes 0.1 second and IOPS becomes the bottleneck because only 10 IOPS are used.
explanation: The Storage performance index page states: writing one 1 MB file takes 0.1 s, bandwidth = 10 MB/s and IOPS = 10; in this case, bandwidth (not IOPS) has a high possibility of becoming the bottleneck.

question: Among the following statements about the L2/L3 link aggregation types, which is incorrect?
options: Layer 2 link aggregation requires all member ports to be Layer 2 Ethernet ports.
For Layer 2 link aggregation, a VLAN virtual interface needs to be created so the aggregation interface can support sending and receiving Layer 3 IP packets.
For Layer 3 link aggregation, the sub-interface of the aggregation interface needs to be created so it can carry packets with VLAN tags.
Layer 3 link aggregation requires all member ports to be Layer 2 Ethernet ports.
explanation: The Link aggregation type page states Layer 3 link aggregation requires all member ports to be Layer 3 Ethernet ports, not Layer 2. The other three options match the page text.

question: Regarding the SCSI bus transfer distance and connection limits, which of the following statement is wrong?
options: Single-ended SCSI has a transfer distance of 6 m.
Differential SCSI has a transfer distance of 25 m.
The SCSI bus supports only 8 or 16 bus addresses.
The SCSI maximum rate currently reaches 20 Gb/s.
explanation: The SCSI limitations page lists 6 m single-ended, 25 m differential, and only 8 or 16 SCSI bus addresses. The Comparison of storage protocols table lists the SCSI current maximum rate as 320 MB/s; 20 Gb/s is the iSCSI maximum rate.

question: Among the following statements about the disk utilization rates of various RAID levels, which is wrong?
options: RAID 0 disk utilization is 100%.
RAID 1 disk utilization is 50%.
RAID 5 disk utilization is (N-1)/N.
RAID 6 disk utilization is (N-1)/N.
explanation: The Comparison of various RAID levels table lists RAID 0 100%, RAID 1 50%, RAID 5 (N-1)/N, and RAID 6 (N-2)/N (double parity bits). Option D understates RAID 6's parity overhead.

question: Regarding the server power supply types and RAID reconstruction, which of the following statements are incorrect?
options: AC regulated power supply provides a stable voltage and frequency.
DC regulated power supply provides a stable DC power supply for the load.
Redundant power supply requires the server to be shut down whenever one supply fails so the other can take over.
When a disk in the RAID fails, a hot spare disk or a new hard disk replaces the failed disk and the RAID reconstruction is performed without affecting system operation.
RAID reconstruction always requires manual administrator intervention to copy data block by block.
explanation: The Introduction to server power supply page states that for redundant power supplies, when one fails the other immediately takes over (no shutdown needed). The RAID reconstruction page states reconstruction is performed without affecting system operation and is triggered automatically when a hot spare or replacement disk is detected, not by manual block-by-block copy.

question: Among the following statements about the iSCSI naming convention and PDU encapsulation, which are wrong?
options: An iSCSI initiator IQN follows the format iqn.<year-month>.<reverse-domain>:<name>, for example iqn.2015-08.com.h3c:linux1.
The iSCSI PDU includes a Basic Header Section (BHS) of 48 bytes, plus optional additional headers, head check, data section, and data check.
Each PDU is encapsulated in a TCP packet and then encapsulated layer by layer for transmission on Ethernet.
iSCSI PDUs are encapsulated in UDP segments because UDP provides lower latency.
The iSCSI port number 3260 is found in the source port field of every iSCSI Ethernet frame, never in the destination port field.
explanation: The iSCSI packet format page shows the PDU is carried over TCP (port 3260), not UDP, and shows port 3260 in the destination-port field of the connecting frame; UDP encapsulation is not used. Options A, B, C are correct per the iSCSI workflow/packet format page.

question: Regarding NTP verification and DHCP system composition, which of the following statements are incorrect?
options: NTP verification is used for networks with higher security requirements; the client and server are configured with password verification and the client only synchronizes with verified devices.
NTP verification requires the configuration of the server to be consistent with the configuration of the client.
A DHCP relay is generally a network device such as a router or a Layer 3 switch.
The DHCP server can only be a dedicated physical appliance and cannot be a network device that provides the DHCP function.
A DHCP client must be statically configured by the administrator before it can request an IP from the DHCP server.
explanation: The System composition of DHCP page defines the DHCP server as a server or network device that provides the DHCP function (not exclusively a dedicated appliance), and defines the DHCP client as a host that needs to dynamically obtain an IP (no static pre-configuration). NTP verification statements A/B match the NTP verification page, and the relay statement C matches the DHCP composition page.

question: Which CAS license is REQUIRED in addition to a CVM license when the deployment scheme uses CVM together with CIC?
options: H3C CAS Cloud Virtualization Manager SRM License
H3C CAS Cloud Intelligence Center License
H3C CAS Disaster Recovery Management License
H3C CAS Migration Tool Integrated License
explanation: The license edition table shows that the CVM + CIC scheme requires both one of the three CVM editions AND the H3C CAS Cloud Intelligence Center License.

question: When mounting the ISO image file from the out-of-band management remote console, which server startup mode and boot item should be set?
options: BIOS legacy mode with boot item 'start from local disk'
UEFI mode with boot item 'start from the image'
BIOS legacy mode with boot item 'PXE network boot'
UEFI mode with boot item 'start from PXE'
explanation: The PDF states the startup mode should be set to UEFI and the boot item to 'start from the image'.

question: On a CVK service server using IP storage (IP SAN), how many 1 GB or 10 GB network interface cards are recommended regardless of two-path, four-path or eight-path CPU configuration?
explanation: The service server hardware table shows 6 NICs are recommended when using IP storage across all CPU path configurations (4 NICs when using FC storage or no external storage).

question: Which is the recommended initiator IQN format for a service server (CVK) configured for IP SAN access according to the host initiator information plan?
options: iqn.2016-06.com.h3c:01:cvm
iqn.1991-05.com.microsoft:cvk01
iqn.2020-01.org.h3c:cas-cvk01
explanation: Per the host initiator information planning table, the management server uses iqn.2016-06.com.h3c:01:cvm and service servers use iqn.2016-06.com.h3c:01:cvkNN (e.g., cvk01, cvk02).

question: After CAS software installation, which vSwitch is configured by default while the others must be manually configured according to the plan?
options: Storage vSwitch is configured by default; management and service vSwitches must be manually configured
Service vSwitch is configured by default; management and storage vSwitches must be manually configured
Management vSwitch is configured by default; storage and service vSwitches must be manually configured
All three vSwitches are pre-configured during installation
explanation: The vSwitch configuration section explicitly states the management vSwitch is configured by default after CAS installation, while storage and service vSwitches must be manually configured according to the plan.

question: When configuring system time on a CAS host via CLI, to set 14:29 on September 30, 2012, which command is correct?
options: hwclock --set 0930142912
explanation: The PDF specifies the format date xxxxxxxxxx where the digits indicate month, day, hour, minute, year. For 14:29 on Sept 30, 2012 the command is exactly date 0930142912.

question: When confirming the Set Up Stateful Failover System dialog box, what value must be entered in the pop-up confirmation dialog before clicking OK?
options: OK
YES
CONFIRM
PROCEED
explanation: The hot standby setup steps state that after configuring the virtual IP, subnet mask and standby IP, the user must enter the parameter CONFIRM in the pop-up dialog box and click OK.

question: When configuring the shared file system heartbeat network on the Host Pool page for an iSCSI shared file system, which address is entered as planned in the example?
options: 192.168.11.0 (management network segment)
192.168.13.0 (service network segment)
192.168.12.0 (storage network segment)
169.254.0.0 (link-local heartbeat)
explanation: The PDF example uses the storage network address 192.168.12.0 in the Shared File System Heartbeat Network dialog box.

question: Which of the following are correct CAS performance specifications?
options: Maximum number of servers supported by a single host pool is 128
Maximum number of virtual machines in a single HA cluster is 2000
Maximum logical CPU supported per physical host is 768
Maximum physical memory supported by each physical host is 16TB
Number of snapshots supported by a single virtual machine is 64
explanation: The performance specifications table lists 128 servers/host pool, 2000 VMs/HA cluster, 768 logical CPUs/host, 16TB physical memory/host. Snapshots per VM is 32, not 64.

question: Which of the following statements about FC storage network zoning and HBA planning are correct?
options: It is recommended to divide a zone with one server HBA port and one storage HBA port
Two ports of a dual-port FC HBA card in the server are respectively connected to different FC switches
A dual-port FC HBA card is installed on each controller of the FC shared storage
Two FC switches must be interconnected via an ISL trunk for redundancy
At least one dual-port FC HBA card is required and multi-path must be configured
explanation: PDF states: zone = one server HBA + one storage HBA, dual-port HBA in server splits across two FC switches, dual-port HBA on each storage controller, two FC switches are NOT interconnected, and the FC storage network requires at least one dual-port FC HBA card with multi-path configured.

question: Which of the following are correct meanings of HA policy parameters when configuring a CAS cluster?
options: Effective minimum node number sets the minimum number of hosts required for high-reliability normal operation
If running hosts drop below the Effective minimum node number, VMs cannot perform HA fault migration
HA resource reservation reserves CPU and memory for HA use
When remaining resources fall below the HA resource reservation ratio, starting/restoring/migrating VMs into the cluster is blocked
Compute DRS and storage DRS are enabled by default and cannot be disabled
explanation: PDF defines Effective minimum node number and HA resource reservation as described in options A-D. Compute DRS and storage DRS are set to 'No' by default and can be configured as required.

question: Which of the following statements regarding CAS VM storage resource planning are correct?
options: It is recommended to use the high-speed hard disk
It is recommended to use the storage volume in smart format
For high IO services, the VM should use a storage volume of block device type
Thin provisioning and thick provisioning disks should be used reasonably
All VM disks must be thin provisioned to maximize storage efficiency
explanation: PDF lists all of A-D as VM storage resource planning recommendations. It says 'reasonably use' thin AND thick provisioning, not that all disks must be thin (E).

question: Which steps must be completed during the 'Server preparation before installation' phase prior to mounting the ISO?
options: Configure the out-of-band management address of the server
Configure the server to the best performance mode
Configure the disk array according to the local storage plan
Install CAStools on the host before booting the ISO
Pre-register the license file on the bare-metal server
explanation: PDF lists the three preparation tasks: configure out-of-band management address, set best performance mode, configure array per local storage plan. CAStools is for VMs and license registration occurs after CAS is installed.

question: Which of the following preparation steps should be performed BEFORE creating a VM template from a powered-off VM to avoid network address conflicts?
options: Disconnect the CD-ROM of the VM
Disconnect the floppy disk of the VM
Set the address information of the operating system to automatic obtaining
Configure a new static IP on the VM before cloning
Configure template storage by adding a Template Pool with a target path
explanation: PDF says before creating a VM template: CD-ROM and floppy disk must be disconnected, the OS address must be set to automatic obtaining (to avoid network address conflict), and template storage must be configured by adding a Template Pool. A new static IP is the opposite of the requirement.

question: Regarding switch aggregation and load-sharing recommendations in CAS networking, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: The management network of the service server uses static link aggregation with active/standby load sharing on the vSwitch side and static aggregation on the peer switch
The service network on the vSwitch uses static link aggregation with advanced load sharing on the vSwitch side and static aggregation on the switch side
It is recommended to use 'active and standby load sharing' or 'basic load sharing', and not recommended to use 'advanced load sharing'
'Advanced load sharing' is the officially recommended mode for all production CAS deployments
explanation: PDF explicitly recommends active-standby load sharing or basic load sharing and NOT recommends advanced load sharing - option D is the opposite of the documented recommendation. Options A and B match the Switch planning table; option C is the explicit PDF note.

question: Among the following descriptions of the CAS root password and stateful failover compatibility constraints, which is incorrect?
options: The root password should not contain spaces
The root password should not contain single quotation marks (') or double quotation marks (")
The root password should not start with a hyphen (-)
The root password must be exactly Cloud@1234 to enable stateful failover
explanation: PDF says the root password must avoid spaces, single/double quotes, or a leading hyphen to prevent stateful failover setup failure - it does not mandate Cloud@1234 (that is the default admin web password, not a hardcoded root constraint).

question: Regarding storage pool locking type selection in CAS, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: There are two options for the Locking Type: distributed locking and hardware-assisted locking
Hardware-assisted locking has specific storage requirements
Hardware-assisted locking requires the storage server to support the CAW feature
Distributed locking is only available on FC SAN and cannot be used with IP SAN storage
explanation: PDF lists distributed locking and hardware-assisted locking as the two options without restricting distributed locking to FC SAN only - option D is an unsupported claim.

question: Among the following descriptions of CAStools VM installation for Windows and Linux, which statements are incorrect?
options: For Windows, double-click the virtual CD-ROM and follow the CAStools Setup wizard
For Linux, mount the CD-ROM, enter the Linux directory of CAStools and run ./CAS_tools_install.sh
After CAStools is installed, click the Disconnect button on the CD-ROM page of the Modify VM dialog box
CAStools is installed via the BIOS Setup menu by pressing F2 during VM boot
For Linux, CAStools is delivered as a .deb package that requires dpkg -i to install
explanation: Options A, B, C are exact PDF instructions. CAStools is NOT installed via BIOS F2 menu, and the Linux install is ./CAS_tools_install.sh from the mounted CD-ROM Linux directory, not a dpkg .deb package.

question: Regarding the recommended NIC count on a CVK service server, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: When no external storage is used, 4 NICs (1 GB or 10 GB) are recommended for a two-path server
When using FC storage, 4 NICs (1 GB or 10 GB) are recommended for a four-path server
When using IP storage, 6 NICs (1 GB or 10 GB) are recommended for an eight-path server
When using FC storage, 8 NICs (1 GB or 10 GB) are recommended for an eight-path server
explanation: PDF service server hardware table shows 4 NICs for both 'No external storage' and 'Use FC storage' across two-/four-/eight-path configurations, and 6 NICs for 'Use IP storage'. 8 NICs is not in the table.

question: Among the following descriptions of CAS VM operating system installation when disk information is not visible, which statements are incorrect?
options: Click the Load Driver (L) button during OS installation to select the corresponding driver
After the VM is installed, the virtual CD-ROM and virtual floppy disk should be disconnected via the Modify VM dialog box
After the VM is installed, the H3C CAS platform automatically obtains accurate CPU and memory utilization without any in-guest agent
Flash the iLO/HDM firmware of the CVK host before attempting to install the guest OS
explanation: PDF says without CAStools the platform CANNOT obtain VM CPU/memory utilization (so C is wrong), and there is no iLO/HDM firmware flash step prescribed before guest OS installation (so D is wrong). A and B are exact PDF instructions.

question: Regarding the "Direct read and write (directsync)" virtual disk cache mode in CAS, which of the following descriptions is correct?
options: Both the host cache and the virtual disk cache are shut down, and data is read and written directly to the physical disk
The host cache is shut down and data is read and written only from the virtual disk cache
The virtual disk write cache is shut down, data is written directly to the disk, and there is only the read cache
Data is first written to the cache and is not immediately synchronized to the disk
explanation: PDF: Direct read and write (directsync) shuts down the host and the virtual disk cache, and directly reads and writes the physical disk. Option B describes none, C describes writethrough, D describes writeback.

question: Regarding the transparent page sharing technology for virtual memory in CAS, which of the following descriptions is correct?
options: It uses 4 KB as the unit to slice the memory, reduces the redundant memory pages of the host to one page, and replaces other pages with pointers
It is installed in CAStools and reclaims memory from idle VMs to give to busy ones on the same host
It swaps memory that has not been used for a long time to a disk file and maps it back when accessed
It dedicates one CPU time slice to garbage-collect duplicate memory blocks across the cluster
explanation: PDF: Transparent page sharing uses 4 KB as the unit to slice the memory, reduces redundant memory pages of the host to one page, and replaces other pages with pointers. Option B describes the balloon driver, C describes memory swapping.

question: When the Smart format is used by a CAS virtual disk, which of the following statements about the qcow2 file structure is correct?
options: The beginning 1 M of the qcow2 file is the file header and the default data-block size is 4 K
The beginning 2 M of the qcow2 file is the qcow2 file header (file size, actual address of the L1 table, snapshot information), and the default data block size is 2 M
The L1 table records the actual address of data blocks directly, with no L2 table involved
The qcow2 file header records only the file size; address information is stored separately in an external index
explanation: PDF: The beginning 2 M of the qcow2 file is the qcow2 file header (records file size, actual address of L1 table, snapshot info); the L1 table records the actual address of the L2 table; the L2 table records the actual address of the data block; default data block size is 2 M.

question: Regarding the operation timeout of the CAStools Agent in CAS, which of the following statements is correct?
options: The operation timeout through the Agent is 30 seconds
The operation timeout through the Agent is 60 seconds
The Agent timeout is 5 minutes, after which the operation is retried automatically
There is no timeout because CAStools communicates over a virtual serial port
explanation: PDF: The operation timeout through the Agent is 60s (60 seconds). If the VM is not operated for a long time, operations such as shutdown and restart will be invalid.

question: Regarding OVF template deployment supported by H3C CAS, which of the following statements is correct?
options: CAS supports OVF templates exported from CAS CVM, VMware vSphere, Oracle VM, and ZTE iECS systems; the current version does not support deployment of OVF templates that contain disk partitions
CAS supports OVF templates only when they are exported from H3C CAS CVM
OVF templates that contain disk partitions are fully supported in the current software version
CAS supports OVF templates from KVM and Hyper-V but not from VMware vSphere
explanation: PDF: CAS supports OVF templates exported from CAS CVM, VMware vSphere, Oracle VM, and ZTE iECS systems. The current software version does not support deployment of OVF templates that contain disk partitions.

question: Which of the following describes the role of the L1 table in the Smart (qcow2) virtual disk?
options: The L1 table records the actual address information of the L2 table
The L1 table records the actual address information of the data block
The L1 table records the file header information including the snapshot information
The L1 table stores user data; the L2 table stores file headers
explanation: PDF: The L1 table records the actual address information of the L2 table. The L2 table records the actual address information of the data block.

question: Regarding host network configuration in CAS, which of the following statements is correct?
options: A host can be configured with multiple gateways, one per vSwitch, and routing information is not supported
A host can be configured with only one gateway, but can add routing information
A host cannot be configured with any gateway because routing is centralized at CVM
Both gateway count and routing entries are unrestricted on a CAS host
explanation: PDF: A host can be configured with only one gateway, but can add routing information. If network information of hosts in a cluster is the same, creation can be performed in the cluster.

question: Which of the following correctly describes the Quick clone method for a CAS VM?
options: Quick clone copies all Level 2 and Level 3 image files in full and is suitable only for backup scenarios
Quick clone reuses the original Level 2 image file (shared) and creates a new empty Level 3 image file for the clone; it is suitable for virtual desktop scenarios
Quick clone first powers off the source VM and then performs a block-level dd copy of all disks
Quick clone removes the Level 1 image file and replaces it with the Level 2 image file of the original VM
explanation: PDF (clone method diagram): Quick clone reuses the original Level 2 image file and starts the cloned VM with an empty Level 3 image file. Quick clone is suitable for virtual desktop scenarios.

question: Regarding the CBT (Changed Block Tracking) backup function in CAS, which of the following statements is correct?
options: CBT computes the md5 of every data block during each backup to detect changes
The full backup only copies valid disk data, and the incremental backup uses the dirty bitmap in the memory to record changed data blocks, replacing md5 value computing of each data block
CBT replaces the dirty bitmap with a full disk scan to find changed blocks
CBT can only be enabled when the backup type is set to Differential
explanation: PDF: The full backup only copies valid disk data. The incremental backup uses the dirty bitmap in the memory to record the changed data blocks, replacing md5 value computing of each data block during the previous backup. This improves efficiency and reduces CPU consumption.

question: Regarding the CAS VM recycle bin behavior when a VM is removed, which of the following statements is correct?
options: Once a VM is removed it is permanently deleted immediately and cannot be restored

After the VM is removed to the recycle bin, it can be manually restored during the preservation period and will be automatically deleted after the preservation period; a write-zero operation will be performed on the data storage file when data is completely destroyed
VMs sent to the recycle bin have their image files deleted immediately; only configuration files remain
The recycle bin retains a VM for a fixed 7 days and the retention period cannot be configured
explanation: PDF: After the VM is removed to the recycle bin, it can be manually restored during the preservation period, and will be automatically deleted after the preservation period. The image file of the VM can be retained or deleted. A write-zero operation will be performed on the data storage file when data is completely destroyed.

question: Which of the following are virtual memory management mechanisms used by CAS to alleviate memory pressure on a host?
options: Transparent page sharing
Balloon driver
Memory swapping
Hyper-threaded NUMA scheduling
Live cluster-wide memory deduplication via vMotion
explanation: PDF lists exactly three virtual memory techniques: Transparent page sharing (4 KB slice + hash + pointers), Balloon driver (CAStools-based reclaim), and Memory swapping (swap to disk file). Options D and E are not in the source.

question: Which of the following functions of CAS Tools (the agent installed inside the VM) are described in the source?
options: Monitor information such as CPU, memory, disk utilization, and NIC traffic
Uniformly manage VM operating system parameters such as VM IP address, account, and password
VM time synchronization
Install the Virtio device driver
Provide kernel-level XTS-AES-256 disk encryption from inside the guest
explanation: PDF: CAS Tools monitors VM (CPU/mem/disk/NIC), uniformly manages OS parameters (IP/account/password), provides VM time synchronization, VM shutdown, and installs the Virtio device driver. Disk encryption is performed by the CVK kernel module, not by CAS Tools.

question: Regarding vSwitch planning recommendations in CAS, which of the following statements are correct?
options: It is recommended to create independent management, service, storage, migration, and backup vSwitches and divide different VLANs
It is recommended to configure network bonds for management, service, and storage vSwitches
vSwitch 0 is the management vSwitch by default
The forwarding mode of a vSwitch supports VEB and VXLAN (SDN)
All five recommended vSwitches must share a single VLAN to simplify routing
explanation: PDF: independent management/service/storage/migration/backup vSwitches with different VLANs are recommended; network bonds are recommended for management/service/storage vSwitches; vSwitch 0 is the management vSwitch by default; forwarding mode supports VEB and VXLAN (SDN). Option E contradicts the VLAN-separation recommendation.

question: Which of the following are valid virtual disk types that CAS can use to provide storage to a VM?
options: iSCSI
RDM
Smart
High speed
qcow3-extended
explanation: PDF Virtual disk type diagram lists iSCSI (block device via IP SAN), RDM (file system via FC SAN), Smart (qcow2 file format), and High speed (RBD block device on Server SAN). "qcow3-extended" is not a CAS disk type.

question: Which of the following are valid Type options in the CAS VM backup configuration ("Type" field)?
options: Full VM
Disk Level
Image-only metadata
Snapshot pointer chain
vRAM dump
explanation: PDF VM backup configuration field "Type": Full VM, Disk Level. (Backup Type is a separate field with Full/Incremental/Differential.) The other three are not in the source.

question: Which of the following statements about the vFirewall (vFW) on CAS VMs are correct?
options: The system supports two vFirewall types: Allowlist and Denylist
Rule types include Ingress and Egress
Rules can be configured for TCP, UDP, and ICMP, as well as common application protocols such as HTTP, HTTPS, DNS, SSH, RDP, and MySQL
The vFirewall policy takes effect globally and the security rules will follow when VMs migrate
When both a vFirewall and an ACL are configured for a VM, the ACL takes effect
explanation: PDF: vFW types = Allowlist/Denylist; rule types = Ingress/Egress; TCP/UDP/ICMP plus DNS/HTTP/HTTPS/SSH/RDP/MySQL etc.; policy takes effect globally and follows the VM on migration. Option E contradicts the rule that the vFirewall takes effect when both are configured.

question: Regarding the CAS migration parameter "Initiative CPU Frequency Underlocking", which of the following statements are correct?
options: After it is enabled, the system reduces the CPU scheduling frequency of a VM to reduce the migration data and accelerate the migration
Enabling this feature might decrease VM performance
It takes effect only when you migrate a running VM by changing its host, or by changing both its host and storage
It applies only to offline migration of shutdown VMs
It is available only when the VM uses local storage
explanation: PDF: After enabling Initiative CPU Frequency Underlocking, the system reduces the CPU scheduling frequency of a VM to reduce migration data and accelerate migration; enabling might decrease VM performance; it takes effect only when migrating a running VM by changing host or changing both host and storage.

question: Which of the following statements about VM Computing QoS (CPU and memory resource QoS) in CAS are correct?
options: When resources are fully competed, resources are allocated based on proportions according to different priorities
The preemption priorities include low, medium, and high, and the default value is medium
Limit defines the upper bound such that the CPU and memory allocated by the CVK to the VM cannot exceed the limit size
The lower limit reservation ensures the lowest service resource guarantee
Priority is fixed at "high" for all VMs and cannot be changed
explanation: PDF Computing QoS: when resources fully competed, allocate by proportion to priorities; preemption priorities are low/medium/high with default medium; Limit caps allocation; lower-limit reservation ensures minimum guarantee. Option E contradicts the configurable priority.

question: Regarding storage QoS in CAS, which of the following descriptions is incorrect?
options: Storage QoS supports limiting the I/O rate, including limiting read and limiting write
Storage QoS supports limiting the IOPS, including limiting read and limiting write
Storage QoS is implemented by applying the network policy template to the VM port on the vSwitch
Storage QoS configuration distinguishes between read and write operations
explanation: Incorrect: PDF Storage QoS supports limiting the I/O rate and IOPS (each with read/write). Option C describes Network QoS (network policy template applied to the VM, speed limit on vSwitch port), not Storage QoS.

question: Regarding the differential backup type in CAS, which of the following descriptions is incorrect?
options: The recovery time is short, and it does not rely on intermediate backup data
Duplicate data exists, and the size of the backup file will continue to increase over time
It is based on the full backup and only backs up data that has changed since the last full backup
It only backs up data that has changed since the last backup (full or incremental), so there is no duplicate data
explanation: Incorrect: D describes incremental backup. PDF: differential backup is based on the full backup and copies data changed since the last full backup; duplicate data exists and the backup file size will increase over time.

question: Regarding CAS virtual disk encryption, which of the following descriptions is incorrect?
options: Kernel-level disk encryption uses the XTS-AES-256 symmetric algorithm and is unrelated to the type of VM operating system
Disk encryption can cause significant VM performance loss and user services will feel obvious read/write delay
Disk encryption supports VM hot encryption; the data key is obtained from the kernel for decryption when starting
When the VM is migrated within the cluster, key information and files are migrated synchronously
explanation: Incorrect: PDF explicitly states "Disk encryption cause no loss to VM performance, and user services have no sense of whether the disk is encrypted." Option B reverses this fact.

question: Regarding online VM migration in CAS, which of the following statements is incorrect?
options: Online migration means migrating the VM in a running state
The image file of the VM must be stored in the shared storage when only the host is changed
Memory data of the VM is transferred from the source host to the target host through the management network
Because the VM keeps running during online migration, the target host can briefly reach the Pause state but the source never does
explanation: Incorrect: PDF (Principle of online host migration - During migration) shows that during online migration the SOURCE VM runs and the TARGET VM is in Pause state until memory data is transferred; the running/Pause roles are the opposite of what D claims. The other three statements (running-state definition, shared storage requirement, memory transfer via management network) match the source verbatim.

question: Regarding the CAS trusted zone for VM data-layer security, which of the following descriptions is incorrect?
options: The trusted zone is a range of hosts in which VMs can be migrated
The virtual secret level must be Secret, Confidential, or Top Secret to be protected; the VM can be migrated and cloned only in the secure zone
Operations such as clone, clone as template / convert to template, and disk unloading on VMs within the protected security level are not allowed
When deleting a VM within the protected security level, the system retains all physical bits of the disk file and never clears them
explanation: Incorrect: PDF states that when deleting a VM within the protected security level, it is mandatory to clear the physical bits of the disk file.

question: Among the following descriptions of the CAS storage path priority and shared file systems, which are incorrect? (Select two)
options: When automatically selecting the storage path, the priority is RBD >> Shared File System >> Local
Shared file system examples include OCFS2 and VMFS
Local file systems include FAT32, NTFS, and EXT2/3/4
Shared file systems allow only one host to access the storage at a time; concurrent host access is not supported
Local file systems support a heartbeat and data synchronization mechanism between multiple hosts
explanation: Incorrect: PDF says a shared file system is read and written in parallel on multiple hosts (concurrent access through heartbeat and data synchronization), and local file systems are directly connected to a single host via system bus (no cross-host sync). D and E reverse the two.

question: Regarding the three CPU working modes in CAS, which of the following statements are incorrect? (Select two)
options: Compatibility mode uses QEMU64 and is the recommended mode
Host matching mode delivers performance close to the host CPU but is poorer in migration compatibility than Compatibility mode

Straight-through mode transparently transmits the host CPU model to the VM, has optimal performance, and has perfect migration compatibility even across different CPU generations of the same manufacturer
Compatibility mode provides hardware-level features such as aes encryption acceleration to the VM
In Straight-through mode, different generations of CPUs from the same manufacturer may not be able to migrate
explanation: Incorrect: PDF says Straight-through mode has very poor migration compatibility and different generations of CPUs from the same manufacturer may not be able to migrate (C is wrong); and Compatibility mode cannot provide optimal performance such as aes encryption (D is wrong).

question: Regarding ACL and vFirewall in CAS, which of the following statements are incorrect? (Select two)
options: The ACL policy supports inbound and outbound access control, and types include IPv4 and Layer 2
ACL policies support effective time setting
vFirewall and ACL can both be enabled on the same VM and their rules are merged; the union of rules applies
When both vFirewall and ACL are configured on a VM, the ACL takes effect and the vFirewall is ignored
When a VM migrates, the vFirewall security rules follow the VM
explanation: Incorrect: PDF says vFirewall and ACL are mutually exclusive; if both are configured the vFirewall takes effect. Therefore C (rules merged) and D (ACL takes effect) both contradict the source.

question: Which of the following correctly describes the local-memory access behavior of NUMA in H3C CAS?
options: Each CPU may only access memory resources within its own NUMA node; cross-node access is blocked by the interconnect module
Local memory resources are preferentially allocated, and when local memory is insufficient the insufficient section is allocated from the remote end
All memory is uniformly accessed by every CPU at the same latency through the NUMA Interconnect module
Remote-end memory is preferentially allocated so that local memory is reserved for the hypervisor
explanation: The PDF NUMA page states 'The local memory is preferentially allocated' and 'The insufficient section is allocated from the remote end' with smaller delay and greater bandwidth on local access.

question: Regarding the H3C CAS memory swapping (disk swap) over-commit method, which statement is correct?
options: It slices memory into 4 KB pages and deduplicates identical pages across VMs
It uses the balloon driver installed in CAStools to release free memory to another VM on the same host
It swaps memory the VM has not used for a long time to a disk file and establishes a mapping relationship, swapping the memory back when the memory is accessed again
It directly maps a persistent memory region onto local storage to accelerate the VM base image
explanation: The PDF memory over-commit page describes memory swapping as 'Swap the memory that the VM has not used for a long time to a disk file, and establish a mapping relationship; and swap the memory back when the memory is accessed again.'

question: Which of the following best describes the H3C CAS disk lock?
options: A centralized locking mechanism providing mutually exclusive locking for cluster nodes through the disk so that cluster mutual exclusion no longer relies on the network
A per-VM exclusive SCSI3 PR reservation set by the guest OS to protect application data
A timeout-based file lock implemented inside the OCFS2 journal without storage-array involvement
A software watchdog that fences a host when the management network is partitioned
explanation: The PDF defines the disk lock as 'a centralized locking mechanism that provides a mutually exclusive locking mechanism for cluster nodes... mutual exclusion of nodes between clusters is no longer through the network but through the disk.'

question: Which of the following best describes the Write Same feature in H3C CAS?
options: A storage feature where the CAS issues a Write Same SCSI command to perform all write-zero tasks; storage only allocates the corresponding space and returns to CAS without performing specific write-zero operations
A host-side cache that batches small writes into one large 1 MB write before issuing them to the array
A CVK feature that compresses repeating byte patterns inside an I/O before transmission to the array
A LUN-level feature that converts all overwrites into copy-on-write snapshots on the array side
explanation: The PDF Write Same page states 'the CAS can issue a Write Same SCSI command to complete all write-zero tasks. The storage only needs to allocate corresponding spaces, and then return to the CAS without performing specific write-zero operations.'

question: Regarding the VM directly mounted RBD block storage path in H3C CAS, which statement is correct?
options: RBD path is mandatory for any FC SAN integration on CAS
The RBD path avoids the I/O performance loss caused by the ocfs2 file system and the iSCSI protocol when CAS is connected to Ceph
RBD requires the underlying storage to be NFS-exported by ONEStor
RBD path removes the need for both CAS and the storage to support it; only CAS support is required
explanation: The PDF VM directly mounted RBD page states 'The RBD path can avoid the I/O performance loss caused by the ocfs2 file system and the ISCSI protocol' when CAS is connected to Ceph.

question: How does CAStools interact with the H3C CAS CVK kernel to determine the running status of the VM operating system for VM blue-screen HA?
options: Through an in-guest IPMI BMC virtualization channel
Through a virtual serial port channel between CAStools and the H3C CAS CVK kernel
Through a network heartbeat between CAStools and the CVM management process
Through a periodic ARP probe on the VM's primary vNIC
explanation: The PDF VM blue screen HA page states 'The CAStools running in the operating system interacts with the H3C CAS CVK kernel through a virtual serial port channel to determine the status of the operating system in real time.'

question: In the H3C CAS Active-Active Stretched Cluster solution, which RPO and RTO objectives are documented?
options: RPO = minute level, RTO = minute level
RPO = 0, RTO = minute level
RPO <= 1 minute, RTO <= 4 hours
RPO = 0, RTO = 0
explanation: The PDF Active-Active Stretched Cluster section states 'RPO=0, minute-level RTO.'

question: Which option correctly lists the four virtualization platforms whose VMs the H3C CAS OVF-based heterogeneous migration supports importing and automatically converting?
options: CAS CVM, VMware vSphere, Oracle VM, and ZTE iECS
CAS CVM, VMware vSphere, KVM, and Xen
CAS CVM, Hyper-V, Nutanix AHV, and Sangfor HCI
CAS CVM, VMware vSphere, Oracle VM, and Huawei FusionCompute
explanation: The PDF OVF heterogeneous platform migration page states it supports 'import and automatic conversion of four virtualization platforms: CAS CVM, VMware vSphere, Oracle VM and ZTE iECS.'

question: Which of the following best matches the H3C disaster recovery indicator definitions for RPO and RTO?
options: RPO measures the time required for the information system to recover from a disaster state to a runnable state, and RTO measures the maximum amount of data loss

RPO measures the maximum amount of data loss allowed by the service system during a disaster, and RTO measures the time required for an information system to recover from a disaster state to a runnable state
RPO and RTO both measure user access recovery objectives at the access edge
RPO measures the recovery cost ceiling, and RTO measures the recovery network throughput target
explanation: The PDF key-indicators page defines RPO as the maximum amount of data loss allowed during a disaster (data redundancy backup capability) and RTO as the time required for the system to recover from a disaster state to a runnable state.

question: According to the H3C disaster recovery level table, which level corresponds to RTO of approximately 1 to 7 days and RPO from several hours to 36 hours?
options: Level 1
Level 2
Level 4
Level 6
explanation: The PDF disaster recovery level table lists Level 2 as RTO 1 to 7 days and RPO from several hours to 36 hours.

question: Which of the following are configuration items defined on the H3C CAS HA configuration page (the main HA enablement page, excluding access-control sub-items)?
options: Tick Enable HA
VM startup priority (set the default startup priority of VMs in the cluster)
Configure HA access control
Trigger actions: failover, VM freezing
Configure the FT primary-standby memory check interval
explanation: The PDF HA configuration page lists exactly these four items: Enable HA, VM startup priority, HA access control, and trigger actions (failover, VM freezing).

question: Which of the following statements about the H3C CAS transparent page sharing and balloon driver memory over-commit methods are correct?
options: Transparent page sharing uses 4 KB as the unit to slice memory, reduces redundant memory pages of the host to one page, and replaces other pages with pointers
When a VM tries to write a shared memory page, a dedicated page is replicated for modification
The balloon driver is installed in the H3C CAStools in the guest OS
The balloon driver releases the free memory in a VM to a VM that needs more memory on the same host
The balloon driver swaps memory pages out to a host disk file when the host runs out of physical memory
explanation: The PDF memory over-commit page describes (0)-(3) for transparent page sharing and balloon driver; swapping to disk is the separate memory-swapping method, not the balloon driver.

question: Which of the following statements about the H3C CAS Active-Active Stretched Cluster best practice are correct?
options: A stretched cluster is deployed at two data centers with a quorum node performing active and standby switchover
As a best practice, use bare optical fibers to connect the two DCs
The distance between two DCs should be not longer than 5 km and the network latency not longer than 1 ms
It is most suitable for two DCs in the same campus or DCs that are close to each other
The solution achieves the best RPO but a high RTO of several hours
explanation: The PDF Active-Active Stretched Cluster page lists exactly (0)-(3); RTO is explicitly stated as 'minute-level', not high several-hour.

question: Which of the following are steps in the H3C CAS MoveSure heterogeneous platform migration process?
options: Download the migration client from the CVM management platform
Install the client on the source device, which connects to the CVM and reports the device status
The target VM mounts the PE image and configures the TargetClient through the PE image boot
The user creates and starts the migration task, and the client obtains the migration instruction
The CAS automatically applies storage snapshots on the source ESXi datastore to freeze the source VM
explanation: The PDF MoveSure migration process page lists exactly the four steps (0)-(3); there is no ESXi-snapshot freezing step.

question: Which of the following are configuration objects required when configuring SRM site disaster recovery on H3C CAS?
options: Site: add the production site and the disaster recovery site and synchronize the asynchronous remote replication relationship and policy
Protection group: divide VMs belonging to the same storage pool into a protection group and configure the vSwitch, network policy template, and storage resource mapping
Recovery plan: specify a unified recovery policy for the protection group
FT pair group: define the active-standby memory mirror pair for each protected VM
VM affinity group: classify VMs by must-run / should-run / must-not-run / should-not-run with the recovery hosts
explanation: The PDF Site disaster recovery configuration page lists exactly Site, Protection group, and Recovery plan as the SRM site DR configuration objects.

question: Which of the following are H3C CAS SRM disaster recovery use scenarios documented in the source?
options: Disaster recovery drill (start the drill / end the drill)
Recovery planning (planned switch when the protection site is maintained)
Failure recovery (real disaster, protection site fails)
Reverse recovery (recover VMs back to the protection site after it is repaired)
Continuous synchronous mirroring across both sites with zero data loss
explanation: The PDF lists exactly these four SRM use scenarios; SRM is based on asynchronous remote replication, so option 4 (synchronous mirroring with zero data loss) is not an SRM scenario.

question: Which of the following are correct statements about H3C CAS DRX dynamic resource expansion configuration steps?
options: Configure DHCP on the switch
Configure the load balance policy on the LB device, and create the virtual service, real service, and real service group
Create the dynamic resource expansion service, bind resources, perform service monitoring and scheduling deployment, and specify the service VM group
Configure SR-IOV VF passthrough on every CVK host that will hold the expansion service VMs
Configure SRM site asynchronous remote replication between the local data center and the disaster recovery data center
explanation: The PDF DRX configuration steps page lists exactly the three steps DHCP, LB policy, and DRX service on the CAS platform; SR-IOV and SRM are not DRX requirements.

question: Regarding the H3C CAS vGPU smart-scheduling principle, which of the following statements are correct?
options: Each vGPU has an exclusive fixed video memory and occupies the entire GPU Engine in its fragment time through a time division multiplexing mechanism
When VMs in a service VM group apply service templates of the same priority, the VM that is started first obtains vGPU resources
When the number of idle vGPUs is less than the number of high-priority VMs to be started, the system releases some resources that have been used by low-priority VMs to guarantee high-priority VMs
vGPUs are assigned strictly by static admin reservation; service template priority and start order have no effect
vGPU video memory is dynamically shared across all VMs through a single pooled hash table
explanation: The PDF vGPU pages state exactly statements (0), (1), and (2); priority-based preemption explicitly exists, and video memory is per-vGPU fixed, not shared.

question: Among the following descriptions of the H3C cloud rainbow function for cross-cloud VM migration, which one is incorrect?
options: The cloud rainbow builds a bridge between clouds so service VMs can be freely migrated online across cloud data centers without service interruption
Cloud rainbow management includes adding, modifying, and deleting CVM cloud resources, and migrating VMs between CVMs
Operation can be done by dragging the VM that needs to be migrated online from the left topology to the destination host on the right topology
Cloud rainbow migration requires the source and destination CVMs to run different major versions to perform a rolling upgrade during the migration
explanation: The PDF cloud rainbow configuration page explicitly requires 'The version of the remote data center CVM to be added is the same as that of the local CVM'; different major versions are not permitted.

question: Regarding the H3C CAS host wakeup methods used by DPM, which of the following statements is wrong?
options: Network wakeup requires enabling the Network Wakeup function in the BIOS of the host (for example, set Wake-On LAN to Enabled on an H3C server)
